report_name: FBI 'Major Incident': China-Linked Hackers Breach Sensitive Surveillance Network

threat_actors.name: Salt Typhoon
threat_actors.description: A sophisticated state-sponsored threat actor linked to China's Ministry of State Security, responsible for breaching the FBI's Digital Collection Systems Network (DCSN) by compromising U.S. telecommunication providers. Their primary objective is intelligence gathering.
threat_actors.goals: Intelligence gathering against U.S. government and critical infrastructure targets, including accessing FBI surveillance data and wiretap information.

attack_patterns.name: External Remote Services
attack_patterns.description: Used as the initial point of entry by exploiting vulnerabilities in internet-facing systems at the targeted telecommunication providers.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Used for lateral movement within the compromised telecommunication networks after gaining initial access.

attack_patterns.name: External Proxy
attack_patterns.description: Used to mask the origin of the attack by chaining through compromised infrastructure.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Information Repositories
attack_patterns.description: The actor accessed sensitive surveillance data, including wiretap returns and PII, which are stored in the FBI's Digital Collection Systems Network (DCSN), a specialized information repository.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: Data was likely exfiltrated over encrypted channels, potentially using web services to blend in with normal network traffic.

attack_patterns.name: Supply Chain Compromise
attack_patterns.description: The attack compromised a critical supply chain partner (U.S. telecommunication providers) to gain access to a downstream target (the FBI's DCSN).

tools.name: PowerShell
tools.description: Used as a living-off-the-land binary (LOLBin) for reconnaissance and lateral movement within sensitive network enclaves.

tools.name: wmic
tools.description: Used as a living-off-the-land binary (LOLBin) for reconnaissance and lateral movement within sensitive network enclaves.

tools.name: netsh
tools.description: Used as a living-off-the-land binary (LOLBin) for reconnaissance and lateral movement within sensitive network enclaves.

campaigns.name: Salt Typhoon U.S. Telecom Campaign
campaigns.description: A multi-year campaign by Salt Typhoon, running from 2019 to 2024, targeting major U.S. telecommunication providers to access call records and lawful intercept infrastructure, culminating in a breach of the FBI's Digital Collection Systems Network.

identities.name: Federal Bureau of Investigation
identities.description: U.S. government agency whose Digital Collection Systems Network (DCSN) was breached by Salt Typhoon. The breach was classified as a 'major incident'.

identities.name: U.S. Telecommunication Providers
identities.description: A class of critical infrastructure entities compromised by Salt Typhoon as a means to access the FBI's surveillance data network.

identities.name: China's Ministry of State Security
identities.description: The Chinese government agency to which the threat actor Salt Typhoon is linked.

identities.name: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency
identities.description: Mentioned in the context of being under a DHS shutdown during the breach, raising questions about national defensive posture.

infrastructure.name: Compromised Lawful Intercept Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Third-party infrastructure within U.S. telecommunication providers that was compromised by Salt Typhoon to pivot and access the FBI's Digital Collection Systems Network.

infrastructure.name: Salt Typhoon Proxy Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: A chain of compromised infrastructure used as external proxies to mask the origin of Salt Typhoon's activities.

indicators.name: Anomalous Login to Lawful Intercept Systems
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Login to Lawful Intercept Systems
id: 2b1f8c9a-7d3e-4b0f-8c9a-1d2e3f4a5b6c
status: experimental
description: Detects anomalous login activity to lawful intercept systems, such as from unusual geolocations or at odd hours, which could indicate compromise by an actor like Salt Typhoon.
references:
  - https://securityboulevard.com/2026/06/fbi-surveillance-network-breached-salt-typhoons-quiet-war-on-american-law-enforcement-infrastructure/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-06
tags:
  - attack.t1078
level: medium
logsource:
  category: authentication
  product: vpn
detection:
  selection:
    is_privileged: true
  filter_domestic:
    user_location|contains: 'US'
  condition: selection and not filter_domestic
indicators.description: This indicator detects anomalous login activity for privileged accounts on lawful intercept systems, such as logins from unusual geolocations or at odd hours, as suggested in the hunting hints.

indicators.name: LOLBin Usage on Sensitive Network Enclaves
indicators.pattern:
title: LOLBin Usage on Sensitive Network Enclaves
id: 8e9d0c1b-5a4f-4d3c-8b2a-9f8e7d6c5b4a
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of living-off-the-land binaries (LOLBins) like PowerShell, wmic, and netsh for reconnaissance or lateral movement within sensitive network segments, such as lawful intercept systems.
references:
  - https://securityboulevard.com/2026/06/fbi-surveillance-network-breached-salt-typhoons-quiet-war-on-american-law-enforcement-infrastructure/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-06
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1047
  - attack.t1059.003
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\netsh.exe'
  filter_known_good:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\explorer.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter_known_good
indicators.description: This indicator detects the use of living-off-the-land binaries (LOLBins) such as PowerShell, wmic, and netsh, which the article suggests hunting for as a sign of reconnaissance and lateral movement.

relationships.source_name: Anomalous Login to Lawful Intercept Systems

LOLBin Usage on Sensitive Network Enclaves
